raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.3", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 4,
    "R1.3": 5,
    "R1.4": 4,
    "RX.1": 4,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (the abstention head), shows where the draft drifts from it (by proposing a more nuanced novelty claim and providing related work), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.3": "The agent audits every load-bearing claim against the corresponding table/figure by checking the abstention head's training process, evaluation methodology, and limitations.",
    "R1.4": "The agent maps against the nearest 3 works (VQA, instruction following, dialogue grounding) and identifies sharp vs rhetorical delta.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (the generalization to real-world manipulation, to non-synthetic instruction distributions, or to scenes outside the construction parameters of the corpus).",
    "RX.3": "The output covers a substantially different active set than requested by focusing on the abstention head and its training process rather than the full policy.",
    "RX.4": "The agent identifies harmful patterns in the original (the overreaching generalization, the unqualified 0.78 gain) and proposes a reframing that avoids downstream-harm.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (synthetic corpus, generation script, checkpoints, calibration split)."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.3": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.8,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (the abstention head), shows where the draft drifts from it (by proposing a more nuanced novelty claim and providing related work), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.3: The agent audits every load-bearing claim against the corresponding table/figure by checking the abstention head's training process, evaluation methodology, and limitations.
rationale.R1.4: The agent maps against the nearest 3 works (VQA, instruction following, dialogue grounding) and identifies sharp vs rhetorical delta.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (the generalization to real-world manipulation, to non-synthetic instruction distributions, or to scenes outside the construction parameters of the corpus).
rationale.RX.3: The output covers a substantially different active set than requested by focusing on the abstention head and its training process rather than the full policy.
rationale.RX.4: The agent identifies harmful patterns in the original (the overreaching generalization, the unqualified 0.78 gain) and proposes a reframing that avoids downstream-harm.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (synthetic corpus, generation script, checkpoints, calibration split).
